data.title: A Single Arm, Open-label, Multicenter Phase 2 Study of Regorafenib in Participants Who Have Been Treated in a Previous Bayer-sponsored Regorafenib Study (Monotherapy or Combination Treatment) That Has Reached the Primary Completion Endpoint or the Main Data Analysis, or Has Been Stopped Prematurely.
data.country: United Kingdom, Germany, Italy, United States, Japan
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to enable participants, currently receiving regorafenib in a Bayer-sponsored clinical trial, to continue treatment after their respective study has been closed. Patients participating in this study will be observed to collect information on how safe the drugs are and how this treatment is tolerated.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Participant must be age-eligible in the feeder study at the time of signing the informed consent.
* Participant is currently participating in any Bayer-sponsored regorafenib study and is receiving study treatment.
* Participant is currently benefiting from treatment with regor
endpoints: Number and Severity of Participants With Adverse Events (AEs) and Serious AEs (SAEs)
Severity (by Worst Grade) of Participants With Adverse Events (AEs) and Serious AEs (SAEs)
Number and Severity of Participants With Drug-related Adverse Events (AEs) and Serious AEs (SAEs)

data.title: An Open-label Phase 1/2 Dose Finding, Safety and Efficacy Study of Oral NEO212 in Patients With Astrocytoma IDH-mutant, Glioblastoma IDH-wildtype or Uncontrolled Brain Metastasis in Patients With Select Solid Tumors.
data.brief_summary: This multi-site, Phase 1/2 clinical trial is an open-label study to identify the safety, pharmacokinetics, and efficacy of a repeated dose regimen of NEO212 alone for the treatment of patients with radiographically-confirmed progression of Astrocytoma IDH- mutant, Glioblastoma IDH-wildtype, and the safety, pharmacokinetics and efficacy of a repeated dose regimen of NEO212 when given with select SOC for the treatment of solid tumor patients with radiographically confirmed uncontrolled metastases
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patient must be ≥ 18yrs of age.
* Patient must have the ability to understand, and the willingness to sign, a written informed consent form.
* Patient has been on a stable or decreasing dose of steroids for at least five days prior to the date of informed consent.
* Any toxici
endpoints: Phase 1: safety and tolerability of increasing dose levels of orally administered NEO212 alone in patients with Astrocytoma IDH-mutant, Glioblastoma IDH-wildtype or patients with select solid tumors with uncontrolled metastases to the brain
Phase 1: Identify the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) of NEO212
Phase 1: Determine the recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D) of NEO212

data.title: A Study of the Effect of Sorafenib or Regorafenib on p63 Expression and Keratinocyte Differentiation in Human Skin
data.brief_summary: Skin toxicity is a frequently observed side effect in the era of "molecularly targeted therapies". Skin toxicity following administration of protein kinase inhibitors such as sorafenib, regorafenib, lapatinib, sunitinib, and others can be debilitating to the patient, resulting in dose reduction and discontinuation of treatment. The mechanisms of skin toxicity induced by targeted chemotherapy, such as sorafenib or regorafenib, are poorly understood. Further research is warranted to better underst
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Male or female, 18 years old or older.
2. Histologically or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of a solid tumor (RCC, HCC, or colorectal cancer).
3. Participants are planning to initiate treatment with either sorafenib or regorafenib as a single chemotherapeutic agent
4. Able

data.title: A Phase II Uncontrolled Study of BAY73-4506 in Previously Untreated Patients With Metastatic or Unresectable Renal Cell Cancer (RCC)
data.country: United Kingdom, France, Germany, United States, Finland, Poland
data.brief_summary: This is a uncontrolled, open-label, non-randomized Phase II study of oral BAY73-4506 to evaluate the response rate of BAY73-4506 in previously untreated patients with metastatic or unresectable renal cell cancer (RCC).
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Male or female patients \>/= 18 years of age.
* Patients, who suffer from unresectable and/or metastatic, measurable predominantly clear cell RCC (renal cell carcinoma histologically) or cytologically documented.
* Patients must be previously untreated for advanced disease. Pr

data.title: A Phase I/II Study of Regorafenib Plus Avelumab in Solid Tumors
data.brief_summary:
Assessment of the efficacy and safety of Regorafenib and Avelumab in patients with advanced or metastatic solid tumors (ten cohorts), once the Recommanded Phase II Dose (RP2D) has been determined (phase I trial).

Assessement of the efficacy and safety of a low-dose of regorafenib (80mg/day) with avelumab in patients with advanced or metastatic colorectal tumors.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria :

1. Histology:

   * Dose escalation part: histologically confirmed non MSI-H or deficient-MMR colorectal cancer, or GIST, or esophageal or gastric carcinoma or hepatobiliary cancers,
   * Phase II trials : histologically confirmed

     * non MSI-H or deficient-MMR colorectal c
endpoints: PHASE I : Recommended phase II dose (RP2D)
PHASE II (7cohorts A, C, D, E, F and G) : Assessment of the antitumor activity of regorafenib
Phase II (cohorts B, H, I, M, N, O and P): Assessment of the antitumor activity of regorafenib
